titleStmt.title: Letter from Henry Arthur Wynne to Philip C. MacDermot, 28 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Philip C. MacDermot

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Sir Henry Arthur Wynne (1867 – 1945). Wynne was appointed to the vacant position of chief crown solicitor following the death of Sir Malachy Kelly (1851-1916). The letter is addressed to Philip MacDermot (b. 1863), the crown solicitor for Roscommon.This is a typed copy of a letter relating to a cattle driving case on the Mather's lands in Ballinsaloe. Wynne notifies MacDermot that the case has been adjourned with the permission of the attorney general, James Campbell (1851 – 1931).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Cattle Driving off Mathers Lands Ballinasloe Prosecutions 28th July 1916 Dear Sir As I wired you the Attorney General consents to
adjournment to 5th August and it is not necessary for you to
go to Ballinasloe toâmorrow but you will instruct the District
Inspector to consent and see that if necessary the Defendants
and Sureties enter into new recognisances to attend on 5th
August I note what you say as to the Defendant Harvey. If he
be arrested in the meantime you can have his case adjourned to
5th August also. As the Defendants are having Counsel the Attorney
General approves of your sending a brief to his Counsel,
Mr John Cusack, 67 Pembroke Road, Dublin. Please do so. Yours truly Henry Arthur Wynne P C P MacDermot Esq
